headline: Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia Results Gap Between SK and SJKC Widens to 12%
context: SPM 2024 results show the average performance gap between national school (SK) and Chinese school (SJKC) primary graduates widened to 12 percentage points, fuelling renewed school integration debates.

cards.big: SJKC primary graduates outperform SK graduates by 12 points at SPM.
cards.sub: The gap has widened from 8 points in 2015. It exists despite SJKC receiving 38% less funding per student.

cards.big: SJKC average class size is 38. SK average is 28.
cards.sub: Larger classes with less funding producing better outcomes suggests systemic factors beyond resources.

cards.big: SJKC emphasises rote learning and homework volume.
cards.sub: Critics call this outdated. Results suggest it produces measurable academic outcomes within the existing exam system.
Parents of all races are choosing SJKC — the market signal is that families value its outcomes.

cards.big: The school receiving less money produces better results. The policy response is not to learn from it but to debate closing it.

Rather than debating school integration, Malaysia should study what SJKC does differently — and apply those practices across all school types. Learning from success is more productive than eliminating it.
